_metadata.description: Energy-sector climate actions extracted from the CityCatalyst High Impact Actions (HIAP) database. Includes all actions where sector = stationary_energy or description/purpose relates to energy generation, distribution, efficiency, or electrification.
_metadata.notes: Action IDs carry prefixes indicating origin: c40_ = C40 Cities, icare_ = ICARE/Brazilian municipal framework, ipcc_ = IPCC-derived. Data sourced from hiap-meed/data/2_action/actions.csv in the CityCatalyst repo (API was not directly reachable from this environment).

c40.action_name: Introduce energy-efficiency standards for new residential buildings
c40.description: The city adopts new energy-efficiency standards for residential construction, ensuring all new buildings incorporate design and performance requirements such as improved thermal insulation, natural ventilation, shading devices, efficient lighting (LED), and solar-ready wiring for PV integration. Standards may also establish region-dependent requirements for cooling demand reduction (e.g., reflective roofs, cross-ventilation) and may include whole life carbon requirements and promote the use of sustainable materials.

c40.action_name: Introduce energy-efficiency standards for new institutional buildings
c40.description: The city introduces energy-efficient and resource-efficient standards for schools, hospitals, and other public buildings. Standards mandate efficient HVAC systems, daylighting strategies, occupancy sensors, reflective roofs, and integration of PV systems where feasible. Includes whole life carbon requirements and sustainable materials promotion.

c40.action_name: Support Implementation of Industrial Building Efficiency Standards
c40.description: Set guidelines for environmentally responsible industrial construction, ensuring new structures adhere to energy-efficient practices, thereby reducing carbon emissions. Standards may include whole life carbon requirements and sustainable materials promotion.

c40.action_name: Retrofit residential buildings for energy efficiency
c40.description: Support retrofitting existing residential buildings — replacing inefficient appliances with high-efficiency models, sealing windows/doors, improving indoor comfort through natural ventilation and shading. Prioritize passive cooling before installing high-efficiency cooling technologies using low-GWP refrigerants.

c40.action_name: Retrofit commercial and institutional buildings for energy efficiency
c40.description: Promote energy retrofits in commercial/institutional buildings — upgrading HVAC systems with VRF units, replacing lighting with LEDs, implementing building automation, and improving thermal envelopes (insulation, reflective coatings, shading).

c40.action_name: Retrofit municipal buildings for energy efficiency
c40.description: Lead by example through energy retrofits in municipal buildings — replacing outdated AC, upgrading lighting to LEDs with smart controls, improving building envelopes, and installing energy management systems.

c40.action_name: Optimize energy efficiency and sustainability standards for existing industrial infrastructure
c40.description: Promote optimization of energy and environmental performance in existing industrial infrastructure. Upgrade equipment, improve process efficiency, and retrofit industrial facilities to higher sustainability standards.

c40.action_name: Promote the phase out of fossil fuels for cooking
c40.description: Promote environmentally friendly cooking practices, including the use of high-efficiency electric cookstoves such as induction stoves, replacing fossil fuel-based cooking.

icare.action_name: Improve heat and energy recovery
icare.description: Enhanced energy efficiency in industrial processes by recovering heat and energy from waste streams, improving process coupling, and adopting advanced technologies such as coke dry quenching, black liquor gasification, and top pressure recovery turbines.

icare.action_name: Promote and expand the use of the Brazilian Energy Labeling Program for Buildings
icare.description: Encourage adoption and enforcement of the Brazilian Energy Labeling Program (PBE Edifica) for buildings — evaluates thermal efficiency, lighting, and HVAC systems. Incentivize developers to seek certification and integrate energy criteria into municipal building codes.

icare.action_name: Develop integrated renewable energy planning and solar-battery microgrids for rural and peri-urban areas
icare.description: Promote integrated regional energy planning combining renewable energy generation (solar PV, wind) with battery storage and microgrid systems to extend reliable electricity access to rural and peri-urban areas where grid expansion is unviable.

icare.action_name: Enable smart grid integration and distributed energy storage
icare.description: Collaborate with energy utilities, regulators, and technology providers to deploy smart grid infrastructure and distributed energy storage systems that enhance flexibility, reliability, and renewable energy integration.

icare.action_name: Deploy cogeneration systems in industries
icare.description: Utilize combined heat and power (CHP) systems to maximize energy efficiency in industrial processes.

icare.action_name: Promote Net-Zero and Green Building Certifications
icare.description: Promote sustainable buildings by supporting net-zero energy goals and encouraging green building certifications (e.g., LEED). Incentivize construction and renovation of buildings that produce as much energy as they consume.

icare.action_name: Expand solar energy generation on municipal facilities and public assets
icare.description: Install and scale up solar PV systems on municipal buildings and public assets — schools, health centers, bus terminals, water treatment plants, and decommissioned landfills. Includes pilot smart meter and automated control projects.

icare.action_name: Promote Mini and Micro-Hydropower and Biomass Energy
icare.description: Develop mini and micro-hydropower plants and encourage biomass energy production in agricultural regions to diversify the energy mix and improve energy security.

icare.action_name: Promote solar thermal and heat pump systems for water heating
icare.description: Encourage adoption of renewable and efficient water heating systems — solar thermal collectors and heat pumps — across residential, commercial, and public facilities. Install in public buildings and provide incentives for private adoption.

icare.action_name: Set municipal energy efficiency targets
icare.description: Establish municipal-wide energy efficiency targets covering all sectors of city operations and the broader community.

icare.action_name: Upgrade and maintain electrical substations
icare.description: Enhance the reliability and efficiency of the electrical grid by modernizing substation equipment and implementing regular maintenance programs.

icare.action_name: Integrate renewables into municipal water systems
icare.description: Implement renewable energy sources (solar, wind) to drive water treatment, pumping, and distribution systems, reducing energy costs and emissions.

icare.action_name: Implement time-of-use electricity tariffs
icare.description: Introduce time-of-use electricity tariffs that incentivize consumers to shift energy use to off-peak hours, helping balance grid demand and integrate renewable energy more effectively.

icare.action_name: Promote passive cooling through natural shading and ventilation in buildings
icare.description: Require or encourage passive cooling design features — shaded facades, green roofs, natural ventilation, reflective surfaces — to reduce dependence on mechanical air conditioning.

icare.action_name: Capture & Use Biogas at Wastewater Treatment Plants
icare.description: Install biogas systems in wastewater treatment facilities to produce renewable energy from organic sludge. Technologies include sludge digestion, CHP for plant loads, and upgrading to biomethane for vehicles.

icare.action_name: Install biodigesters in rural properties
icare.description: Equip rural farms with biodigesters to convert organic waste into biogas and nutrient-rich fertilizers, providing a sustainable energy source.

icare.action_name: Improve Fuel Supply Systems for Industrial Heating
icare.description: Enhance fuel delivery systems in industrial production by implementing pulverized coal injection, improving furnace designs, and optimizing fuel combustion. Includes potential adoption of alternative reductants like biomass.

icare.action_name: Enhance industrial energy efficiency processes
icare.description: Implement advanced technologies and process optimizations in industrial facilities to minimize energy consumption and improve productivity.

icare.action_name: Deploy energy-efficient and solar-powered street lighting across all neighborhoods
icare.description: Install LED-based, solar-powered streetlights in all neighborhoods including rural and peri-urban areas. Prioritize solar power generation in areas with limited grid coverage or high carbon intensity.

icare.action_name: Promote Solar Photovoltaic Energy Generation in Public and Distributed Systems
icare.description: Promote and scale solar PV generation across public facilities and distributed energy systems to increase the share of renewable electricity.

icare.action_name: Develop regional hubs for renewable energy production
icare.description: Establish strategic centers for large-scale renewable energy generation, supporting local economies and providing reliable, clean energy to surrounding areas.

icare.action_name: Increase efficiency of industrial furnaces and kilns
icare.description: Enhance furnace and kiln design and operation — improved heat and energy recovery from process gases, advanced process controls, and better designs to minimize temperature cycles.

icare.action_name: Improve Heat Recovery and Reuse through Heat Exchanger Networks or Heat Pumps
icare.description: Improve heat-use energy efficiency using heat exchanger networks and high-temperature heat pumps. Includes thermal insulation, waste heat reuse, and power generation via mechanical vapor recompression.

icare.action_name: Adopt industrial energy efficiency technologies
icare.description: Implement energy-efficient technologies in mining and manufacturing — upgrading ore concentration methods, efficient grinding/crushing, and optimizing separation, drying, and materials handling.

icare.action_name: Electrify Industrial Processes (with Renewable Power)
icare.description: Transition industrial production to electrified methods powered by low-carbon electricity — including molten oxide electrolysis and advanced direct reduction.

icare.action_name: Decarbonize industrial feedstocks
icare.description: Decarbonise feedstocks through CCS, CCU, and integration with low-carbon energy sources — capturing carbon from industrial emissions and reusing or storing it.

icare.action_name: Implement carbon capture and utilization (CCU)
icare.description: Capture CO2 from industrial processes and reuse for producing fuels, chemicals, and building materials. Prioritize CCU for residual emissions with robust MRV.

icare.action_name: Implement carbon capture and storage (CCS)
icare.description: Capture CO2 from industrial processes, energy generation, or the atmosphere and store in geological formations. Apply CCS primarily to residual/process emissions after efficiency and electrification measures.

icare.action_name: Adopt hybrid renewable energy systems (solar-wind)
icare.description: Combine solar and wind energy technologies to create reliable, diversified renewable energy systems suitable for various environmental conditions.

icare.action_name: Reduce technical losses in energy distribution networks
icare.description: Upgrade and maintain electrical infrastructure to minimize energy lost during transmission and distribution, improving overall grid efficiency.

icare.action_name: Implement energy efficiency criteria in municipal procurement
icare.description: Require all renovations and constructions contracted by the municipality to minimize artificial lighting and cooling, allow natural light and cross ventilation, and include sustainable materials and passive design.

icare.action_name: Replace Inefficient Lighting with LED Technology in Public Spaces
icare.description: Replace outdated lighting systems in public spaces with energy-efficient LED technology to enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and lower carbon emissions.

ipcc.action_name: Stimulate solar energy production in industrial facilities
ipcc.description: Encourage the installation of solar energy systems in factories to reduce dependence on fossil fuels and minimize associated emissions.

ipcc.action_name: Encourage the Use of Zero-Emission Fuels in Industrial Processes
ipcc.description: Promote transition to zero-emission energy sources in industrial processes — green hydrogen, biogas from sustainable sources, and synthetic e-fuels produced with renewable electricity.

ipcc.action_name: Transition to renewable energy for industrial operations
ipcc.description: Encourage the shift to renewable energy sources (wind, solar, hydroelectric) in industrial operations to reduce fossil fuel use.
